name: Eirene
description: Eirene, also known as Pax in Roman mythology, is the Greek goddess of peace. She is one of the Horae, goddesses of the seasons and the natural order. As the personification of peace, Eirene represents the abundance and prosperity that follow peace. She is the daughter of Zeus, king of the gods, and Themis, the personification of divine law and order. This parentage emphasizes her connection to justice and the established order as prerequisites for lasting peace. Eirene is often depicted as a beautiful young woman holding a cornucopia, symbolizing the bounty of the earth, or with a scepter and torch, representing the power and light of peace. Sometimes she is shown with the infant Plutus, the god of wealth, indicating that peace brings prosperity. Her role in Greek society was significant, especially during times of war, as she represented the ideal state of harmony and well-being that all cities strived to achieve. Eirene’s presence signified the end of conflict and the beginning of a period of stability and growth. She was worshipped in Athens, where a statue of her holding the infant Plutus was erected in the Agora. The statue represented the hope for prosperity through peace. Her influence extended beyond religious worship, as she embodied the political and social aspirations of a peaceful and thriving society. Eirene's absence was felt keenly during times of strife, highlighting her importance in maintaining the balance of the world.
subtitle: Goddess of Peace and Prosperity

keyMyths.title: Birth of Eirene
keyMyths.description: Eirene is born from Zeus and Themis, symbolizing peace originating from divine order. Her parentage highlights the importance of justice in achieving lasting peace.

keyMyths.title: Eirene and Plutus
keyMyths.description: The statue of Eirene holding the infant Plutus represents the prosperity that follows peace. It signifies that peace brings abundance and wealth to the land.

keyMyths.title: Worship in Athens
keyMyths.description: Eirene was worshipped in Athens, especially during times of war. Her worship represented the hope for a return to peace and prosperity after conflict.
